stem: A healthcare study measures average patient recovery rate across 5 hospitals, each containing multiple departments. hospital A has 10 observations with mean 64.2 % (within-hospital SD = 9.7). hospital B has 8 observations with mean 52.3 % (within-hospital SD = 7.3). hospital C has 15 observations with mean 25.4 % (within-hospital SD = 8.1). hospital D has 10 observations with mean 60.7 % (within-hospital SD = 6.7). hospital E has 5 observations with mean 38.8 % (within-hospital SD = 6.1). The between-hospital standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.3 and 18.3 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average patient recovery rate for hospital C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-hospital variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for hospital C: 26.0 % (shrunk from raw 25.4). Range [25.7, 27.0] as between-hospital SD varies from 7.3 to 18.3. Pooled mean: 46.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 46.7 % (weighted average of 5 hospital means).
Shrinkage factor for hospital C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=12.2: B = 0.971.
Interval [25.7, 27.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (25.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (46.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
